content.mi:
Te Whāngai i te Kaitiaki

Ka haere ngā kuia o Ngāti Ngahere ki te taha o te wai me ā rātou kete iti kī tonu i te kai.

Ka maka māhaki rātou i te kai ki te waha o te ana.

“Noho ora, e Te Tahi,” ka kōrero hū rātou.

Be well, Te Tahi.

Ko ētahi e kī ana he koha.

Ko ētahi e kī ana he tohu whakaute.

Ā, ko ētahi e mea ana he tikanga kua mau tonu mai i ngā rā o nehe.
content.en:
Feeding the Guardian

The kuia of Ngāti Ngahere would walk to the water’s edge carrying small baskets of kai.

They would toss the food gently into the cave’s mouth.

“Noho ora, e Te Tahi,” they whispered.

Be well, Te Tahi.

Some said it was a gift.

Some said it was a sign of respect.

And some said it was simply the way things had always been.
